items.title: Aussie’s Slide Versus Kiwi to Slow on Rate Gap, Strategists Say
items.description: The Australian dollar’s steep selloff against its New Zealand counterpart is set to slow, with analysts saying Australia’s higher interest rates and a key technical support will cushion the currency.

items.title: Bond Traders Take Warsh at Word, See Inflation Fight Continuing
items.description: Bond traders and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh are in agreement on a crucial point: The central bank’s fight against inflation still seems far from over.

items.title: Tropical Storm Watches Posted Across Florida’s Panhandle Region
items.description: A tropical storm watch has been issued for the Florida Panhandle as the Atlantic’s next event takes shape off shore.

items.title: Extreme Stock Swings Tempt Funds Into Reverse Dispersion Trade
items.description: Betting that individual stocks will be volatile while the S&P 500 stays relatively calm has been a popular and successful hedge fund strategy. But with the swings in share prices reaching extreme leve

items.title: Prediction Markets Swell to 27% of Sports Bets During World Cup
items.description: Prediction-market trading has surged during a blowout World Cup, far outpacing the growth at traditional sportsbooks and driving home the competitive threat that companies like Kalshi now pose to the

items.title: Safest Carry in Emerging Markets Is in Latin American Currencies
items.description: Emerging-market currency volatility has dropped to the lowest since the start of year and that’s re-energizing carry trades, particularly those involving Latin America that are seen to offer the juici

items.title: Caspian Pipeline Consortium Halts Loadings on Drone Strike
items.description: Oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium’s terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast was suspended after a drone strike, prompting the Kazakh government to call for an immediate end to such attacks.

items.title: Iran Conflict Escalates as US Announces Third Troop Death
items.description: The US and Iran engaged in a quickening series of tit-for-tat attacks, causing fresh chaos in the Middle East as the US military announced the death of a third US troop in the past two days.

items.title: Jingye Says It Reserves Legal Rights in UK Steel Nationalization
items.description: Jingye Steel Co. has reserved all legal rights, including international arbitration, over the UK government’s nationalization of British Steel.

items.title: Singapore Eyes Hedge Fund Tax Cuts to Boost Hub Role: FT
items.description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore has held talks with investment firms on reducing taxes for fund managers in a bid to maintain the island’s competitiveness and retain talent, the Financial Times re

items.title: South Korea Details Plan for Foreigners to Trade Won Easier
items.description: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the forex market and bring the currency closer to full convertibility.

items.title: Venezuela’s Official Earthquake Death Toll Rises to 5,119
items.description: The official death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month rose to 5,119, according to National Assembly head Jorge Rodríguez.

items.title: Korea’s AI-Heavy Market Now Sets the Tone for Global Stocks
items.description: For fund managers in London, New York and Tokyo, a new ritual has emerged before trading gets underway: checking South Korean stocks.
